verdicts.issue: Key year 2004 mismatches entry year 2002 by 2 years (>1-year tolerance per rule §5). The book is the 2002 second edition of Appel/Palsberg 'Modern Compiler Implementation in Java'. Either change year to 2004 (no edition published that year) or rename key. Recommend renaming key to appel2002tiger; rename requires updating every [@appel2004tiger] reference in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc).

Key prefix 'banbury' mismatches actual first author 'Reddi, Vijay Janapa'. The author list begins with Reddi, not Banbury (Banbury is co-author #7). Per rule §5 surname must match. Recommend renaming key to reddi2021widening; rename requires updating every [@banbury2021widening] reference in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc). Alternative: keep as project-name key if intentional.
Co-instructor name is wrong: bib has 'Zheng, Zico' but actual co-instructor is J. Zico Kolter. Author should be 'Chen, Tianqi and Kolter, J. Zico'.

suggested_fix.author: Chen, Tianqi and Kolter, J. Zico

verdicts.issue: Key year 1989 mismatches entry year 2018 by 29 years (>1-year tolerance per rule §5). The Routledge 2018 reissue is a reprint of the 1989 chapter in Resnick (ed.), 'Knowing, Learning, and Instruction' (Lawrence Erlbaum). Either change year back to 1989 (original Erlbaum publisher) or rename key. Recommend year=1989 with publisher Lawrence Erlbaum Associates.
Author field has malformed LaTeX diacritics: 'Sepp{"a}l{"a}, Otto' uses unbalanced quotes (will render as literal {'a}). Should be 'Sepp\"{a}l\"{a}, Otto' or 'Sepp{\"a}l{\"a}, Otto'.

suggested_fix.author: Ihantola, Petri and Ahoniemi, Tuukka and Karavirta, Ville and Sepp{\"a}l{\"a}, Otto

verdicts.issue: Key prefix 'kannan' does not appear in author list at all (first author is Won, William; co-authors include Heo, Rashidi, Sridharan, Srinivasan, Krishna). Year mismatch: key 2022 vs entry 2023 (1-year, silent). Per §5 the surname token is wrong. Recommend renaming key to won2023astrasim2 (matches mlsysim/paper bib); rename requires updating every [@kannan2022astrasim] reference in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc).
Key year 2025 mismatches entry year 2026 by 1 year (silent under §5 strict rule, but inconsistent with key naming). Project-name key — minor.

Key year 2004 mismatches entry year 2009 by 5 years (>1-year tolerance per rule §5). The Pintos SIGCSE paper was published in 2009 (ACM SIGCSE Bulletin Vol 41, Issue 1). Recommend renaming key to pfaff2009pintos; rename requires updating every [@pfaff2004pintos] reference in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc).

Key year 2013 mismatches entry year 2011 by 2 years (>1-year tolerance per rule §5). The ITiCSE 2011 paper 'a multi-classroom report on the value of peer instruction' is a 2011 publication. Recommend renaming key to porter2011multi; rename requires updating every [@porter2013multi] reference in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc).

Schema mismatch: entry is @inproceedings but the booktitle is 'arXiv preprint arXiv:1811.02883' and publisher is 'arXiv' — that is not a real conference proceedings. SCALE-Sim 2018 was an arXiv preprint only (the ISPASS paper is the 2020 follow-up by Samajdar et al.). Should be @misc or @article with archiveprefix=arXiv.

Key prefix 'schneider' does not appear in author list at all (sole author is 'Rush, Sasha'). Per §5 the surname token is wrong. Recommend renaming key to rush2020minitorch; rename requires updating every [@schneider2020minitorch] reference in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc). Alternative: keep as project-name key minitorch2020.
Could not independently verify. Likely correct (Australasian Computing Education Conference 2008 proceedings, Bloom's taxonomy paper by Thompson et al.) but did not search.

Schema redundancy: entry has both `journal` and `booktitle` fields with the same NeurIPS venue. As @article with NeurIPS proceedings it should be either @inproceedings (booktitle, publisher Curran Associates) or remove the booktitle field. Per rule §3 NeurIPS publisher should be 'Curran Associates'.

suggested_fix.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)

verdicts.issue: The 'Dive into Deep Learning' work is an open-access book project, not a CoRR/Cambridge journal article. The entry mixes journal=CoRR with publisher=Cambridge University Press — these are inconsistent. The Cambridge edition (2023) and arXiv preprint (2106.11342) are different artifacts; pick one.
